data.reasoningText:
The project uses Microsoft.Testing.Platform (via `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`). To produce a TRX report with the new testing platform, you need to add the TRX report extension package. With MSTest 3.8.0 and the new testing platform, TRX reporting requires `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport`.

Let me add the package and run the tests.

toolResult.sessionLog:
diff --git a/tmp/sv-61213458621b4ce5aaece1b02400e1fb/TestProject.csproj b/tmp/sv-61213458621b4ce5aaece1b02400e1fb/TestProject.csproj
index 0000000..0000000 100644
--- a/tmp/sv-61213458621b4ce5aaece1b02400e1fb/TestProject.csproj
+++ b/tmp/sv-61213458621b4ce5aaece1b02400e1fb/TestProject.csproj
@@ -9,6 +9,7 @@
   <ItemGroup>
     <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.13.0" />
     <PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="3.8.0" />
+    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport" Version="1.6.0" />
   </ItemGroup>
 
 </Project>

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run tests with TRX via platform args

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run tests with TRX via platform args

data.reasoningText:
The `--report-trx` flag isn't recognized by `dotnet test` in SDK 9.0.x. With Microsoft.Testing.Platform, TRX reporting is enabled differently. Let me try the correct approach: either using `--report-trx` as a platform argument or by passing it through `--`. Let me check the correct syntax.

With `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`, you pass testing platform arguments after `--`. So the command would be:

```
dotnet test -- --report-trx
```

Let me try that.

arguments.description: Run tests with TRX via platform args
